headline: GOLD $4,642, SILVER $74 — U.S. Signals New Middle East Strikes; Oil Tops $120
snippet: Gold futures hit $4,642/oz and silver rose to $74 on April 30 after reports the U.S. is weighing renewed military strikes in the Middle East to reopen the Strait of Hormuz, pushing oil above $120/barrel. Both metals gained 2–3% on the session as investors fled to safe-haven assets.

headline: GETCHELL GOLD ADDS 54%: Fondaway Canyon Crosses 999Koz Indicated in Nevada Gold Belt
snippet: Getchell Gold (CSE: GTCH) published an updated MRE for its Fondaway Canyon project in Nevada showing a 54% jump in Indicated resources to 22.1Mt at 1.40 g/t Au (999Koz), plus a further 1.8Moz Inferred. The growth sets up the project for an initial economic assessment as gold trades at all-time highs.

headline: Radisson Drills O'Brien Gold to 1.9km Depth — 4.54 g/t Au in Project's Deepest Hole
snippet: Radisson Mining (TSXV: RDS) extended high-grade gold at its O'Brien project in Quebec's Abitibi to 1,900m vertical depth — the deepest hole ever drilled on the property — returning 4.54 g/t Au over 12.0m with mineralization open below. A 140,000m step-out program with seven active rigs is ongoing.

headline: USGS CONFIRMS: Appalachians Hold 2.3M Tonnes of Lithium — 328 Years of U.S. Imports
snippet: A new USGS survey estimates the Appalachian region — spanning Maine, New Hampshire, and the Carolinas — holds 2.3 million metric tonnes of undiscovered, economically recoverable lithium, enough to power 130 million EVs or replace 328 years of U.S. imports at current rates. The finding significantly reframes domestic critical mineral supply strategy.

headline: HYCROFT RESOURCES UP 55%: 16.4Moz Gold + 562Moz Silver — $189M Cash, Zero Debt
snippet: Hycroft Mining (Nasdaq: HYMC) filed its Q1 2026 10-Q showing M&I resources surged 55% to 16.4Moz gold and 562.6Moz silver, anchored by an initial 90.2Moz high-grade underground silver resource at its Vortex zone in Nevada. The company carries $189M in cash with no debt, funding a two-rig expansion of the Vortex drill program.

headline: FireFly Metals Hits 70.8m @ 4.0% CuEq at Green Bay — PEA on Track for Mid-2026
snippet: FireFly Metals (ASX/TSX: FFM) reported post-quarter drilling at Green Bay in Newfoundland hitting 70.8m at 4.0% CuEq and 53.3m at 4.1% CuEq within its 50.4Mt M&I resource at 2.0% CuEq. A resource update and Preliminary Economic Assessment are targeted for mid-2026; the company also closed the A$86.1M sale of its Pickle Crow gold assets to concentrate capital on Green Bay.

headline: SILVER DEFICIT DEEPENS: Year Six Shortfall Widens 15% to 46M Oz as Juniors Mobilize
snippet: Silver posted its sixth consecutive structural supply deficit in 2026, with the shortfall widening 15% to 46.3 million troy ounces as solar, EV, and defence demand outstrips flat mine output. The U.S. committed $10 billion to a new strategic silver reserve through Project Vault; junior miners including Hycroft, Blackrock Silver, and Aya Gold & Silver are accelerating drill programs in response.

headline: World Bank Sees a Ceiling on Gold and Silver — Warns Rally May Stall Through Year-End
snippet: The World Bank flagged price ceilings for gold and silver through 2026, citing sticky inflation, rate-hike risk re-emerging in the wake of the Middle East conflict, and stretched speculative positioning. The institutional caution lands as both metals trade near multi-decade highs driven by safe-haven demand and central bank buying.

headline: CONTRARIAN: 'Everyone Agrees It's a Crisis. Gold Still Declines.' — Bearish Signals Mounting
snippet: One market analyst argues that despite near-universal consensus around geopolitical and monetary 'crisis,' gold and mining stocks are flashing bearish technical signals that suggest a meaningful correction is due before any next leg higher. The piece cautions retail investors against chasing the narrative at historic price levels.

headline: USA RARE EARTH'S $2.8B BET: Buying Brazil's Pela Ema Mine — Masterstroke or Overpay?
snippet: USA Rare Earth (Nasdaq: USAR) agreed to acquire Serra Verde Group's Pela Ema mine in Goiás, Brazil for ~$2.8 billion, gaining immediate production of all four critical magnetic rare earth elements — Nd, Pr, Tb, Dy — to feed its Oklahoma magnet factory while the Round Top mine awaits completion in 2028. Motley Fool examines whether the strategic premium is justified.

headline: Discovery Silver Takes 9.9% Stake in Abitibi Metals Via C$30.75M Strategic Financing
snippet: Abitibi Metals (CSE: AMQ) secured up to C$30.75 million in a non-brokered financing led by Discovery Silver, which will hold a 9.9% strategic stake upon close. The deal signals institutional conviction in Abitibi's B26 polymetallic project in Quebec and comes as sophisticated buyers are targeting quality junior assets at scale.
